title: Scheduled job skips records created near midnight boundary
bug_description: A scheduled processing job misses records created near the midnight boundary because the time window is calculated incorrectly.
expected_behavior: All records created inside the intended processing window should be included, including those near date-boundary edges.
actual_behavior: Records created just before or after midnight fall outside the computed window and are never processed.

preconditions: Scheduler tests can inject a fixed clock or timezone-aware fixtures
There are fixture records created around 23:59 and 00:01
steps: Run the scheduled job with the clock fixed at a day-boundary time
Inspect which fixture records are selected for processing
Compare the selected record IDs with the expected window
reproduction.expected_behavior: Boundary records inside the intended window are processed exactly once.
reproduction.actual_behavior: At least one valid boundary record is skipped.

logs: Scheduler log shows a computed window start or end that excludes boundary records

manual_checks: Run the job with a fixed clock and confirm all expected boundary records are processed
Confirm records outside the window are still excluded

rollback.rollback_rule: Rollback on any hard-check failure or if the new logic begins reprocessing records outside the intended window.
rollback.notes: Keep the last passing scheduler boundary patch ready for restore.

scope_notes: Limit the fix to window calculation, scheduler selection, and tests around date boundaries. Do not redesign the whole job orchestration layer.

notes.root_cause_hint: The boundary bug may come from timezone conversion, inclusive/exclusive comparisons, or truncation to local midnight.
notes.risk_hint: A naive fix could stop skipping records but create duplicate processing on later runs.
similar_bugs: inclusive end-date check drops midnight record
